data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Simplifies decision-making processes by reducing choices on interface screens for faster user interaction.
data.technical_spec: User interfaces are designed to present a limited number of logical paths.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Ensures that clickable targets are appropriately sized and positioned for quicker user access.
data.technical_spec: Interface elements sized and spaced according to their importance to minimize user effort.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Design decisions consider cognitive load by chunking content to process information more easily.
data.technical_spec: Content organized into easily digestible sections with clear headings.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Interface follows established web conventions to meet user expectations and enhance usability.
data.technical_spec: Design leverages patterns from familiar platforms for intuitive navigation.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Visually appealing interfaces create a positive perception of usability and engagement.
data.technical_spec: Use of responsive, clean designs with high contrast and pleasing visuals.
